content_preview: Abstract

Mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) chondrogenesis is modulated by diverse biophysical cues.
We have previously shown that brief, low-amplitude pulsed electromagnetic fields
(PEMFs) differentially enhance MSC
chondrogenesis. MSCs on TCP or the ALN scaffolds exhibited greater resting mechanical stress and accordingly, were unresponsive, or negatively responsive, to PEMF exposure
from all directions. The efficacy of PEMF-induced MSC chondrogenesis is hence regulated

responses, culminating in a final cellular response. The combined contributions of
micromechanical environment and magnetic field orientation hence will need to be
considered when designing magnetic e
Keywords: pulsed electromagnetic fields, mesenchymal stem cells, electrospun fibres, chondrogenesis, mechanotransduction
